messages.content:
Read the following statements, then decide if they are TRUE or FALSE. 
 STATEMENT 1: As a Masters student at WMG, I must evaluate the extent to which my argument holds true. To evaluate my argument is to recognise the limitations in order to defend what I have done. 
STATEMENT 2: As a Masters student at WMG, I must synthesise the arguments of different authors together. To synthesise is to bring together different ideas.

Question 3 Answer

A.
BOTH statements are FALSE

B.
Statement 1 is FALSE, Statement 2 is TRUE

C.
BOTH statements are TRUE.

D.
Statement 1 is TRUE, Statement 2 is FALSE
B. Statement 1 is FALSE, Statement 2 is TRUE

You want to improve the quality of critical thinking in your assignment. Of the following options, select what you can do to improve the analysis.
Question 4 Answer

A.
Provide an example to illustrate the model in practice

B.
Select one solution and justify why it is the most appropriate given the conditions of the case

C.
Compare and contrast different theories for their strengths and weaknesses

D.
Describe what the different terminologies mean
C. Compare and contrast different theories for their strengths and weaknesses

Read the following paragraph, then select which option is correct. 
 [1] WMG students must develop their professionalism. [2] Professionalism is a Warwick Core Skill that includes “manag[ing] priorities and time” and being “prepared to operate autonomously” (Tissington, 2020:1). [3] One of the ways WMG students develop professionalism is by managing their schedules so they can balance the time requirements to successfully pass their modules, assignments, and project. [4] Therefore, when students do not develop their time management skills, this can undermine their ability to achieve their degree. [5] Hence, WMG students must develop their professionalism to help them achieve their degree.

Question 5 Answer

A.
Sentence [2] is an example of ‘understanding’ from Bloom’s Revised Taxonomy. Sentence [3] is an example of 'understanding’ from Bloom’s Revised Taxonomy

B.
Sentence [2] is an example of ‘understanding’ from Bloom’s Revised Taxonomy. Sentence [3] is an example of ‘applying’ from Bloom’s Revised Taxonomy

C.
Sentence [2] is an example of 'applying’ from Bloom’s Revised Taxonomy. And sentence [3] is an example of ‘analysing’ from Bloom’s Revised Taxonomy

D.
Sentence [3] is an example of ‘analysing’ from Bloom’s Revised Taxonomy. Sentence [5] is an example of ‘understanding’ from Bloom’s Revised Taxonomy
B. Sentence [2] is an example of ‘understanding’ from Bloom’s Revised Taxonomy. Sentence [3] is an example of ‘applying’ from Bloom’s Revised Taxonomy

Read the following paragraph, then select which option is correct.
[1] WMG graduates are “committed to an active contribution to a sustainable world” (Tissington, 2020:1). [2] This is evidenced in the professional development of students’ knowledge of and behaviours for sustainability in SPA. [3] Sustainability is linked to ethical values as living ethically does involve acting with consideration for the environment and society. [4] Therefore, WMG graduates have developed their ethical values as they developed their sustainability.
Question 6 Answer

A.
Sentence [1] is an example of ‘understanding’ from Bloom’s Revised Taxonomy. Sentence [3] is an example of ‘applying’ from Bloom’s Revised Taxonomy.

B.
Sentence [2] is an example of ‘understanding’ from Bloom’s Revised Taxonomy. Sentence [3] is an example of ‘applying’ from Bloom’s Revised Taxonomy.

C.
Sentence [2] is an example of ‘applying’ from Bloom’s Revised Taxonomy. Sentence [3] is an example of ‘analysis’ from Bloom’s Revised Taxonomy. 

D.
Sentence [3] is an example of ‘applying’ from Bloom’s Revised Taxonomy. Sentence [4] is an example of ‘analysing’ from Bloom’s Revised Taxonomy.
D. Sentence [3] is an example of ‘applying’ from Bloom’s Revised Taxonomy. Sentence [4] is an example of ‘analysing’ from Bloom’s Revised Taxonomy.

After you have searched the Library, write the Harvard reference (i.e. the full reference as found in a Reference List) for your 1 chapter in an edited book (this can be an e-book). 
Below your Harvard reference, justify in 2-3 sentences why this source is credible. For example, why would you include this source in an assignment?

Harvard Reference:
Author(s). (Year). Title of chapter. In Editor(s) (Ed.), Title of book (pp. Page range). Publisher.

Justification:
This source is credible because it is a chapter in an edited book, which suggests that it has gone through a comprehensive review process. Edited books typically include contributions from multiple experts in the field, ensuring that the information provided is reliable and authoritative. Additionally, the inclusion of page numbers and publisher information further enhances the credibility of the source.

Read the following scenarios, then select the answer that does NOT lead to an increase in the level of critical thinking in both scenarios.
 Scenario 1 Eden has referenced two figures that evidence the increasing cost of living in the UK and is not sure what to do next.
 Scenario 2 Harper has included an image that illustrates an example of poor digital branding and is not sure what to do next

Question 8 Answer

A.
In Scenario 1, Eden can describe what the figures mean to the reader. In Scenario 2, Harper can express what qualities make the example one of poor branding

B.
In Scenario 1, Eden can analyse what is missing from both figures. In Scenario 2, Harper can describe what problems of branding are visible in the supporting image

C.
In Scenario 1, Eden can evaluate how the cost of living crisis is impacting business. In Scenario 2, Harper can suggest ways to improve the digital branding

D.
In Scenario 1, Eden can provide an additional figure to evidence the cost of living crisis in the UK. In Scenario 2, Harper can evaluate the credibility of the image

D. In Scenario 1, Eden providing an additional figure to evidence the cost of living crisis in the UK does not necessarily lead to an increase in critical thinking. Similarly, in Scenario 2, Harper evaluating the credibility of the image does not inherently involve critical thinking.

Read the following statements, then decide if they are TRUE or FALSE. 
STATEMENT 1: As a Masters student at WMG, I must analyse academic sources in support of my answer. To analyse means I give an example of a key idea presented in a source in the real world. 
 
STATEMENT 2: As a Masters student at WMG, I must evaluate the credibility of sources prior to including them in my assignment. To evaluate the credibility of sources means I weigh the trustworthiness of the content of the source, and the quality of the source itself, to decide whether it is appropriate to include.

Question 9 Answer

a.
BOTH statements are TRUE

b.
BOTH statements are FALSE.

c.
Statement 1 is TRUE, Statement 2 is FALSE.

d.
Statement 1 is FALSE, Statement 2 is TRUE.
a. BOTH statements are TRUE

How might you evidence a higher level of critical thinking in your work?
Question 10 Answer

A.
Citing and analysing a number of different perspectives.

B.
Describing a particular theory in detail.

C.
Recalling key terminology.

D.
Quoting lots of difference sources, even if they are not closely related to my topic.
A. Citing and analysing a number of different perspectives.

Select the option that best orders the sources below based on their credibility. The most credible source is listed first and the least credible source is listed at the end.
 -   Instagram post by a conspiracy theorist published yesterday
-    Peer reviewed article published in an academic journal published in the last two years
-    Edited textbook published nine years ago by the University of Oxford
-    UK government report published in the last year


Question 11 Answer

A.
Peer reviewed article, UK government report, edited textbook, Instagram post

B.
Peer reviewed article, edited textbook, UK government report, Instagram post

C.
Instagram post, edited textbook, peer reviewed article, UK government report

D.
Edited textbook, peer reviewed article, UK government report, Instagram post
A. Peer reviewed article, UK government report, edited textbook, Instagram post

Select the option that best orders the sources below based on their credibility.  The most credible source is listed first, and the least credible source is listed at the end. 
 -     A PhD thesis published this year by WMG
-      An academic textbook published two years ago by Routledge
-      A set of statistics about the national economy published in a report by Statista two years ago
-      A conference abstract published five years ago for an internationally renowned academic conference

Question 12 Answer

A.
PhD thesis, academic textbook, conference abstract, statistical data

B.
PhD thesis, academic textbook, statistical data, conference abstract

C.
Academic textbook, conference abstract, PhD thesis, statistical data

D.
Academic textbook, PhD thesis, statistical data, conference abstract
B. PhD thesis, academic textbook, statistical data, conference abstract
